name: tags-input
description: A free-text TAGS field (Filament TagsInput; shadcn composes Badge + Input): committed tags shown as removable chips + a text input to add more, with optional suggestions. The committed tags POST as a REPEATED `name` via one hidden <input> per chip, so the set submits with zero JS. JS-OFF an explicit Add submit button (name=`<name>__add`) and a per-chip remove submit (name=`<name>__remove`) round-trip to the server, which appends / drops the tag and re-renders -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer makes the editing in-place: Enter / comma / Add appends a chip + hidden input without a round-trip, the chip x deletes it, Backspace-on-empty pops the last, and suggestions are a quick-pick; an aria-live region announces changes. The hidden inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: labelled add-input, aria-labelled remove buttons, live announcements. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.
docs: Copies the tags-input partial to your JTE root at lievit/tags-input.jte (reached as @template.lievit.tags-input(name=..., values=...)) and the CSP-clean enhancer to lievit/tags-input.enhancer.ts. Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllTagsInputs() on load (and after any DOM swap). The committed tags POST as a repeated `name` (a hidden input per chip). JS-OFF the user adds via the Add submit (`<name>__add`) and removes via the per-chip submit (`<name>__remove`), both server round-trips; handle those parameters server-side to append / drop. JS-ON the enhancer adds on Enter / comma / Add and removes via the chip x, all in place, and hides the Add button. Pass `values` (committed tags), optional `suggestions`, `placeholder`, `addLabel`, plus `label` / `ariaLabel` / `describedBy` / `disabled` / `invalid`. The source is yours: edit freely.
